content:
Member-only story

Featured

A field-tested roadmap to building internal .NET SDKs that keep legacy apps — and your sanity — alive.

4 min read
Just now

--

“You touched the billing service? On a Friday?!”
That was our CTO, eyes wide, when I merged the SDK branch. The keyword refactored pulsed in my head like a neon sign. Three days later, prod still hummed. Stick around and I’ll show you exactly how.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Why Even Bother Building an Internal SDK?
Remember that ancient monolith at Acme Corp? We nick-named it “Franken-App” — stitched-together SOAP, REST, and XML-RPC bits that only spoke in grunts. Feature teams spent 40% of sprint time rewiring the same HTTP calls. An internal SDK slashed that to 5% and cut incident tickets in half. JetBrains’ 2025 .NET Survey reports 61% of teams use internal libraries to enforce standards.
public interface IFrankenClient // 👻 centralizes ugly endpoints
{
Task<Customer> GetCustomerAsync(Guid id); // hides XML-RPC
Task SaveOrderAsync(Order order); // wraps legacy SOAP
// ...more gateways
}The Anatomy of a Bullet-Proof SDK Layer
1. Surface Only Clean…
